Popup_CompleteDemoPopupDesc:
Thank you for playing the demo!
We hope this little journey brought some joy to your day.

TBH : Task Bar Hero will be released in Q4 2025. We'll return with a more polished experience.

Add it to your wishlist and support us!
Thank you :)

ResetData_Notice1Text1:
Deletes all play data.
If you proceed with consent, all data will be completely reset and this cannot be undone.

- Since TBH uses Steam server data, recovery after reset is impossible.
- The developer is not responsible for any data you directly consent to delete.
ResetData_NeedCheckNotice: Please review all of the above.
ResetData_AgreeResetData: I agree to delete all data.
ResetData_WarningGameQuit:
※ Warning: Data is being deleted.
Do not close the game.
ResetData_ReallyWantReset:
If you really want to reset, press and hold the reset button
for 5 seconds.

※ Note: After resetting, you cannot recreate your account for up to 1 hour.

Description_Hero101: A melee guardian holding the line with sword and shield. Takes enemy onslaught head-on with high HP and defense.
Description_Hero201: A ranged marksman with nimble footwork and sharp archery. Strikes vital points from a safe distance.
Description_Hero301: An AoE dealer who channels mana through staff and orb to ignite the battlefield. Wipes out swarms in an instant.
Description_Hero401: A supporter who heals and protects allies with holy power. Shines in drawn-out battles.
Description_Hero501: A tactical hunter wielding crossbow and traps. Dominates terrain and distance to relentlessly pressure even strong foes.
Description_Hero601: A berserker who cleaves everything with an axe. Attacks grow fiercer the greater the danger.

Notice_Delete_OnlyInventoryItem_PopUpTex: TBH is a server-based game where all items can be traded on the Steam Market.
When server items and local save files do not match due to errors or manipulation below, automatic sync is triggered.
1. Cloud save sync error\n2. Response delay due to network issues\n3. Items created through abnormal means
Please keep Steam Cloud Save enabled so that your save file stays up to date.
If an issue occurs due to network problems, please restart the game and check your mailbox.
For any other unexpected issues, please contact us on the official Discord or at help@nugemstudio.com.

UI_DataCollectGuide_Context:
In <TBH: Task Bar Hero>, the following information is collected to enable Steam Market use and to ensure a fair gameplay environment.

[Information collected on first access | Purpose: account identification and login authentication]
- SteamID
- App ID
- Steam authentication session ticket (one-time login token)

[Information automatically collected by the game server when using online features | Purpose: operation and stability of online services, account security, and prevention of cheating and unauthorized access]
- IP address and IP-based country information
- Device information and device-based language information
- OS information
- Nickname
- Access (connection) date and time (including logins)

[Game data collected to provide online features | Purpose: server storage of game progress data, server-side verification of item grants and exchanges, and client version compatibility checks]
- Inventory data (item ID, item instance key, chest cooldown)
- Item acquisition, consumption, and exchange (synthesis / opening) request records
- Game client version (for compatibility checks)
- Game progress data: data that constitutes the user's game progress state (e.g., character level)

[Collected only upon cheat detection | Purpose: detecting cheating and maintaining a fair gameplay environment]
- SteamID and game version
- Current and highest stage progress, cumulative playtime
- Game progress data: data that constitutes the user's game progress state (e.g., character level)
- Detection rule ID and related detection parameters

For more details, please review our Terms of Service.
(https://store.steampowered.com//eula/3678970_eula_1)

If you do not wish to play the game, please exit the game.

TermsOfService_Content:
TBH: Task Bar Hero
Terms of Service & Privacy Policy

Applies to
This version of the Terms applies to Users who have consented to this version
through the in-client consent process. This version corresponds to the
server-based client (Ver 1.00.12 and later). Users who have not consented to
this version remain governed by the version of the Terms to which they last
consented, until they consent to this version. Users with no recorded consent
to any version must consent to the then-current version in order to use the
Game.
Developer: Nugem Studio & Tesseract Studio
Contact: help@nugemstudio.com

IMPORTANT NOTICE
Please read these Terms carefully before playing TBH: Task Bar Hero.
When you first launch a client that requires this version, you will be asked to
review and consent to these Terms. Consent is required to use the online
(server-based) features. The version you consent to is recorded on your account.
If you do not consent, you may continue to use offline features or
previous-version features under the terms to which you last consented, but you
may not use online features that require the current version.

=================
TERMS OF SERVICE
=================

Article 1 (Purpose)

These Terms of Service (hereinafter "Terms") govern the rights, obligations,
responsibilities, and personal data processing between Nugem Studio and
Tesseract Studio (hereinafter "Company") and users in connection with the use of
TBH: Task Bar Hero (hereinafter "Game").

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 2 (Definitions)

The terms used in these Terms are defined as follows.

1.  "Game" refers to TBH: Task Bar Hero and all related services provided by the
    Company.

2.  "User" refers to any person who explicitly agrees to these Terms via the
    consent process and uses the Game.

3.  "Minor" refers to any person under the age of 18 under the laws of the
    Republic of Korea. For users in the European Union, "Minor" additionally
    refers to any person under the age of 16 (or such lower age as permitted by
    the applicable EU Member State law, but no lower than 13). For users in
    other jurisdictions, the age threshold defined by applicable local law shall
    apply.

4.  "Legal Guardian" refers to a parent or legal guardian of a Minor.

5.  "Game Item" refers to digital content generated or acquired through the
    in-game network features.

6.  "Marketplace" refers to the Steam Community Market, where users may trade
    Game Items using Steam Wallet funds.

7.  "Game Data" refers to all data generated during gameplay, including play
    logs, item holdings, and transaction history.

8.  "License" refers to the limited, non-exclusive, revocable right to use the
    Game.

9.  "Personal Information" refers to information that identifies or can identify
    an individual.

10. "Platform" refers to the Steam service operated by Valve Corporation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 3 (Effectiveness and Amendment of Terms)

1.  These Terms become effective when the User explicitly consents by checking
    the consent checkbox and clicking the confirmation button upon launching the
    Game. Users who do not consent may not proceed to use the online
    (server-based) features of the Game.

2.  These Terms become effective immediately upon first publication. The Company
    may amend these Terms within the scope permitted by applicable laws. Any
    subsequent amendments will be announced at least 7 days prior to the
    effective date via in-game notices or Steam announcements.

3.  If a User does not agree to the amended Terms, the User may discontinue use
    of the Game. Continued use after the announcement of amendments shall be
    deemed as consent to the amended Terms.

4.  Consent-version-based application: This version of the Terms applies to
    Users who have consented to this version through the in-client consent
    process. The version a User consents to is recorded on the User's account.
    Use of the online (server-based) features requires consent to the
    then-current version. A User whose recorded consent version is not the
    current version will be asked to review and consent to the current version
    at the next online login, before using online features. A User who does not
    consent may continue to use offline features or previous-version features
    under the terms to which the User previously consented, but may not use
    online features that require the current version. A User with no recorded
    consent to any version must consent to the then-current version in order to
    use the Game. References to a specific client version (Ver 1.00.12 and
    later) are informational only, indicating the client to which this version
    of the Terms corresponds, and do not by themselves determine which version
    of the Terms applies to a User.

5.  Relationship to the notice period: The advance-notice period in Article 3(2)
    applies only to amendments that the Company implements without renewed
    consent. Where a new version is applied through the User's active in-client
    consent, that version takes effect for that User at the moment of consent,
    and the notice period in Article 3(2) does not apply to, and does not delay,
    such consent-based changes.

6.  Effect of declining consent: Online features — including server-stored
    inventory, item generation, and Marketplace-related features — are provided
    only to Users who have consented to the then-current version of the Terms.
    A User who declines may use only offline features or previous-version
    features, and cannot access server-stored items or online features. Before
    declining, a User may request a copy of their personal data held by the
    Company in accordance with Article 6 of the Privacy Policy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 4 (Use by Minors)

1.  Minors must obtain consent from their Legal Guardian prior to using the
    Game. For EU/EEA users, the applicable age thresholds defined in
    Article 2(3) above apply.

2.  If a Minor uses the Game without the consent of their Legal Guardian, the
    Legal Guardian may cancel such use. However, cancellation may be restricted
    if the Minor misrepresented themselves as an adult.

3.  Legal Guardians may request the Company to review a Minor's game usage
    history and impose usage restrictions.

4.  The Company implements measures to protect Minors in accordance with
    applicable laws, including youth protection regulations and, where
    applicable, the GDPR (for EU/EEA users).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 5 (License and Intellectual Property)

1.  The Company grants Users a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license
    to use the Game for personal, non-commercial purposes. This license may not
    be assigned, sold, leased, or sublicensed.

2.  Users may not modify, reverse engineer, reproduce, or distribute the Game.

3.  All copyrights and intellectual property rights to content within the Game
    (including graphics, music, sound, code, characters, story, and UI) belong
    to the Company.

4.  Users are permitted to share content created in relation to the Game (such
    as screenshots and videos) for non-commercial purposes. Commercial use
    requires prior written consent from the Company.

5.  The Company may terminate the License after notifying the User in the event
    of a Terms violation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 6 (Service Provision and Changes)

1.  The Company may modify game content, systems, and item structures as
    operationally required.

2.  Updates may change the performance or value of existing items, and no
    separate compensation will be provided for such changes. However, the
    Company will provide advance notice of significant changes. Rights
    guaranteed to Users under applicable mandatory laws (such as consumer
    protection laws) are not limited by this paragraph.

3.  Some features of the Game require a network (internet) connection.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 7 (Steam Account and Platform)

1.  This Game is provided through the Steam platform operated by Valve
    Corporation.

2.  Users are responsible for the security of their Steam accounts. The Company
    is not responsible for damages resulting from account theft or hacking,
    unless caused by the Company's own fault.

3.  Valve Corporation's terms of service take precedence with respect to Steam
    platform policies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 8 (Network Features and Game Item Generation)

1.  The Game uses a network (internet) connection for online features. Online
    features — including account login, inventory, item generation, and item
    transactions — are processed and stored on the Company's dedicated game
    server, which is operated through a processor (see Privacy Policy Article 3).

2.  Game Items generated through the network are digital content within the Game
    and do not represent real-world assets or investment instruments.

3.  The Company is not responsible for item generation failures caused by
    unstable network connections, server maintenance, or force majeure events.

4.  Users must not use unauthorized methods (such as hacks, macros, or
    third-party programs) during item generation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 9 (Marketplace Transactions)

1.  Game Items may be traded on the Steam Marketplace (Steam Community Market)
    using Steam Wallet funds.

2.  Marketplace transactions are governed by Valve Corporation's Steam Terms of
    Service and Marketplace policies. The Company does not directly intervene in
    Steam platform trading policies.

3.  The Company is not responsible for profits, losses, or disputes arising from
    transactions, which are handled under Steam platform policies.

4.  Abnormal transactions, deliberate price manipulation, and fraudulent
    activities are strictly prohibited and may result in usage restrictions upon
    detection.

5.  The Company may modify item attributes for economic balance purposes and is
    not responsible for changes in trading value resulting from such
    modifications. Rights guaranteed to Users under applicable mandatory laws
    are not limited by this paragraph.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 10 (Refund Policy)

1.  The Game is provided free of charge, and refunds do not apply to the Game
    itself.

2.  Refunds for DLC (Downloadable Content) are handled entirely in accordance
    with Valve Corporation's Steam Refund Policy, and the Company has no
    authority to process refunds directly.

3.  Users wishing to request a DLC refund should do so directly through Steam
    Customer Support at store.steampowered.com/support.

4.  Steam Wallet transactions on the Steam Marketplace are also subject to
    Valve Corporation's policies, and the Company has no authority to process
    refunds for such transactions.

5.  Consumer rights guaranteed by applicable mandatory laws (such as consumer
    protection laws and EU Consumer Rights Directive) are preserved regardless
    of these Terms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 11 (User Obligations)

1.  Users must not engage in the following activities:
    - Using unauthorized methods such as hacks, macros, or auto programs
    - Exploiting in-game bugs or vulnerabilities
    - Price manipulation, fraud, or abnormal transactions in the Marketplace
    - Infringing upon the Company's intellectual property rights
    - Using Game content for unauthorized commercial purposes
    - Violating applicable laws and regulations

2.  The Company may operate automated systems to detect fraudulent activities.

3.  Upon confirmation of a violation, the Company may take the following
    actions:
    - Warning and prior notice
    - Temporary suspension of game access
    - Item recovery
    - Permanent ban from the Game

4.  Users may appeal the Company's sanctions by submitting a request to
    help@nugemstudio.com. The Company will notify the User of the outcome
    within 14 days of receipt.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 12 (Service Changes and Suspension)

1.  The Company may modify or suspend service content as operationally or
    technically required.

2.  In the event of a service suspension, the Company will provide at least
    30 days' advance notice via Steam announcements. However, post-hoc notice
    may be given in cases of force majeure.

3.  Upon service termination, the Company will provide at least 30 days' prior
    notice via in-game announcements and Steam. During this notice period, users
    may request a copy of their personal data held directly by the Company by
    contacting help@nugemstudio.com.

4.  Game Items and Game Data will be deleted upon service termination. The
    Company bears no obligation to compensate for such deletion unless otherwise
    required by applicable mandatory law.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 13 (Disclaimer)

1.  The Company is not liable for service interruptions caused by force majeure
    events such as natural disasters, war, hacking, or server failures.

2.  The Company is not liable for damages caused by the User's own fault.

3.  The Company is not liable for service changes resulting from policy changes
    by Steam or Valve Corporation.

4.  The Company's liability for damages is limited to the maximum extent
    permitted by applicable laws.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 14 (Governing Law and Dispute Resolution)

1.  These Terms shall be interpreted and applied in accordance with the laws of
    the Republic of Korea.

2.  In the event of a dispute arising from the use of the Game, the Company and
    the User shall in principle resolve it through mutual consultation.

3.  If no agreement is reached, the competent court shall be determined in
    accordance with the Civil Procedure Act.

4.  For international users, applicable mandatory local laws — including EU
    consumer protection laws — may take precedence over these Terms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 15 (Miscellaneous)

1.  Matters not specified in these Terms shall be governed by applicable laws
    and the Steam platform's terms of service.

2.  If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid under applicable law,
    the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

3.  The Company does not use advertising cookies or behavioral-analytics SDKs
    within the Game. Technical data necessary for the operation and security of
    the online service is processed as described in the Privacy Policy. Steam's
    built-in platform telemetry is governed by Valve Corporation's Privacy
    Policy.

4.  Language: These Terms are provided in multiple languages. In the event of any
    discrepancy in interpretation between the translated versions, the English
    version shall prevail.

5.  Previous versions: Previous versions of these Terms and the Privacy Policy are
    archived and remain available to Users at:
    https://docs.google.com/document/d/1b9wt29PHDeek6lXvZSwF0wjZZs2LY1xXMMa6w8q2ZxQ/edit?usp=sharing

=================
PRIVACY POLICY
=================

Applies to: Users who have consented to this version through the in-client
consent process. This version corresponds to the server-based client
(Ver 1.00.12 and later). Users who have not consented to this version remain
governed by the version of the Privacy Policy to which they last consented,
until they consent to this version.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 1 (Information We Collect and Legal Basis)

TBH: Task Bar Hero follows a data minimization policy: the Company collects only
the data necessary to operate the game's online features, secure user accounts,
and prevent abuse. The Company does not use advertising cookies or
behavioral-analytics SDKs; technical data necessary for the operation and
security of the online service is processed as described in this Privacy Policy.

With the introduction of a dedicated game server (operated through a processor;
see Article 3), online features such as account login, inventory, and item
transactions are processed and stored on the server. The data below is collected
accordingly.

[1. Collected on account login / first access — applies to all online users]
  Items       : - Steam ID
                - App ID
                - Steam authentication session ticket (one-time login token)
  Purpose     : Account identification and login authentication.
  Legal Basis : Contract performance (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b))

[2. Automatically collected by the game server when using online features]
  The following is automatically recorded by the game server (via the processor's
  SDK) each time the Game connects to the server.
  Items       : - IP address and IP-based country information
                - Device information and device-based language
                - OS information
                - Nickname
                - Access (call) date and time, including login
  Purpose     : Operation and stability of the online service; account security;
                prevention of abuse and unauthorized access.
  Legal Basis : Contract performance (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)) and legitimate interests
                in security and abuse prevention (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f))

[3. Game data — collected to provide online features]
  Items       : - Inventory data (item IDs, item instance keys, chest cooldown)
                - Item acquisition, consumption, and exchange (synthesis / opening)
                  request records
                - Game client version (for compatibility verification)
                - Game progress data: data constituting the User's saved game
                  state (e.g., character level, in-game currency balances,
                  stage progress). Items within this category may be added or
                  changed as game content evolves, and data in this category
                  is processed only within the scope of storing and validating
                  game progress. If the Company begins to collect a new
                  category of data of a different nature, this will be
                  announced through an amendment to this Privacy Policy.
  Purpose     : Server-side storage of game progress; server-side validation of
                item grants and exchanges; client version compatibility.
  Legal Basis : Contract performance (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b))

[4. Collected only upon cheat detection]
  Collected ONLY when the anti-cheat system detects the potential use of cheat
  software or other abnormal activity. It is NOT collected during normal play.
  Items       : - Steam ID and game version
                - Current and highest stage progress
                - Cumulative playtime
                - Game progress data at the time of detection (e.g., character
                  level, in-game currency balances)
                - Detection rule ID and related detection parameters
  Purpose     : Detection of cheating and maintaining a fair play environment.
  Legal Basis : Legitimate interests — fraud prevention and fair play
                (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f))

Note: Providing the data in Section 1 is required to use the online service.
Without a Steam ID and login authentication, server login is not possible and
online features cannot be provided.

Note: Steam Marketplace transactions are processed on the Steam platform; the
related transaction records are held by Valve Corporation, not by the Company.
(See Article 5.)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 2 (Purpose of Collection)

Collected information is used solely for the following purposes:
  - Account identification and login authentication
  - Provision and stable operation of online features (inventory, item
    transactions, game progress storage)
  - Server-side validation of item grants and exchanges
  - Account security and prevention of abuse / unauthorized access
  - Detection of cheating and maintaining a fair play environment
  - Client version compatibility verification
  - Responding to sanction-related appeals and legal disputes

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 3 (Processing Entrustment)

To operate the online service, the Company entrusts the processing of personal
data to the processors below. The Company enters into a data processing agreement
with each processor as required under GDPR Art. 28.

[Processor 1]
  Entrusted to : AFI Inc. (Backnd, thebackend.io) — Republic of Korea
  Tasks        : Game account authentication; storage of inventory and game data;
                 server-side computation.

[Processor 2]
  Entrusted to : Google LLC (Google Apps Script) — United States
  Tasks        : Operation of the Company's data-collection endpoint for user
                 statistics and cheat-detection logging.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 4 (Retention Period and Destruction)

Personal information is retained only for the period necessary, in accordance with
the data minimization principle (GDPR Art. 5(1)(e)), and is destroyed as follows.

[Account and game data]
  Retention : Retained while the account is active. When a User withdraws (resets)
              their account, game / user data is deleted immediately, and personal
              information is deleted within 7 days of withdrawal.

[Residual withdrawal records]
  Items     : UUID, sign-up date, last access date, and OS / device information at
              last access.
  Retention : Retained after withdrawal only until deleted during the Company's
              periodic deletion process, which is carried out at least once every
              90 days.

[Server request logs]
  Retention : Automatically deleted 30 days after the date of the request.

[Cheat-detection data]
  Retention : Collected only upon cheat detection. Retained for up to 1 year from
              the date of detection, solely to process the resulting sanction and
              any related appeal, after which it is deleted without delay.
  Note      : During this retention period, the right to erasure (GDPR Art. 17)
              may be restricted under GDPR Art. 17(3)(e) and the Company's
              legitimate interests (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)) in establishing,
              exercising, or defending sanctions and related legal claims. Other
              rights — including access, restriction of processing, and objection
              — remain available. (See Article 6.)

[Steam ID (account identifier)]
  Retention : Retained for the duration of active service use; deleted within 30
              days of service termination.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 5 (Third-Party Disclosure and International Transfers)

1.  The Company does not provide personal information to third parties without the
    User's consent, except as required by applicable law. (Entrustment of
    processing under Article 3 is distinct from third-party provision.)

2.  For Steam platform integration, Steam ID and game session authentication data
    are shared with Valve Corporation (acting as an independent data controller
    under its own Privacy Policy). No additional personal data is transferred to
    Valve beyond what is technically required for platform operation.

3.  Steam Marketplace transactions are processed on the Steam platform; the related
    transaction records are collected and held by Valve Corporation under its own
    Privacy Policy, not by the Company.

4.  International transfers:
    - Data processed by AFI Inc. (Backnd) is stored in the Republic of Korea. For
      EU/EEA users, transfers to the Republic of Korea are covered by the European
      Commission's adequacy decision (2021), which recognizes Korea as ensuring an
      adequate level of data protection.
    - Data processed by Google LLC is transferred to the United States under the
      EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework and/or Standard Contractual Clauses, as
      permitted under GDPR Chapter V.
    - Transfers to Valve Corporation (US) are conducted under Valve's Standard
      Contractual Clauses or equivalent mechanism.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 6 (User Rights)

[6-1. Rights regarding data held directly by the Company]

For personal data held directly by the Company (including your Steam ID, account
and game data, and any data collected upon cheat detection), Users may exercise
the following rights by contacting help@nugemstudio.com:

  - Right of Access (GDPR Art. 15)
    Request a copy of your personal data held by the Company.

  - Right to Rectification (GDPR Art. 16)
    Request correction of inaccurate personal data.

  - Right to Erasure / "Right to be Forgotten" (GDPR Art. 17)
    Request deletion of your personal data. Users may also delete their account
    directly through the in-game account withdrawal (reset) feature.
    Exception: Where cheat-detection data has been collected, erasure of that
    data may be restricted for up to 1 year from the date of detection, pursuant
    to GDPR Art. 17(3)(e) and the Company's legitimate interests in enforcing and
    defending sanctions. The data is deleted once this purpose no longer applies.

  - Right to Restriction of Processing (GDPR Art. 18)
    Request restriction of processing in certain circumstances.

  - Right to Data Portability (GDPR Art. 20)
    Request your data in a machine-readable format.

  - Right to Object (GDPR Art. 21)
    Object to processing based on legitimate interests.

The Company will respond within 30 days of receipt of a valid request.

[6-2. Rights regarding data held by Valve Corporation (Steam platform)]

Steam Inventory data and Steam account data are stored and managed by Valve
Corporation as an independent data controller. For these data, Users should
submit requests directly to: store.steampowered.com/contact

[6-3. Right to lodge a complaint]

EU/EEA Users have the right to lodge a complaint with their local Data Protection
Authority (DPA) if they believe their data protection rights have been violated.
A list of EU DPAs is available at:
edpb.europa.eu/about-edpb/about-edpb/members_en

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 7 (International Users)

1.  EU/EEA Users: All rights under the GDPR (Regulation (EU) 2016/679) are
    guaranteed. Legal bases for each category of data processing are specified in
    Article 1 above. International transfers are conducted only where an adequacy
    decision or appropriate safeguards are in place, as described in Article 5.

2.  California (US) Users: Rights under the CCPA are guaranteed, including the
    right to know, right to delete, and right to opt out of the sale of personal
    information. The Company does not sell Users' personal information to third
    parties.

3.  Users in Other Regions: Applicable local personal data protection laws may
    apply.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 8 (Joint Controllers and Privacy Officer)

[8-1. Joint Controllers (GDPR Art. 26)]

Nugem Studio and Tesseract Studio are co-developers of the Game and jointly
determine the purposes and means of the processing of personal data under this
Privacy Policy, acting as joint controllers within the meaning of GDPR Art. 26.
Their respective roles are as follows:

  - Nugem Studio     : Responsible for the distribution and operation of the
                       Game, serving as the contact point for Users, and
                       handling data subject rights requests.
  - Tesseract Studio : As co-developer, jointly participates in determining the
                       purposes and means of processing in the course of game
                       development.

In accordance with GDPR Art. 26(3), Users may exercise their rights against
either company, irrespective of the arrangement above. The designated contact
point for prompt handling is Nugem Studio.

[8-2. Privacy Officer]

For inquiries regarding personal data processing, please contact us below.

  Privacy Officer : Data Protection Division, Nugem Studio
  Email           : help@nugemstudio.com
  Response Hours  : Weekdays 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (KST)
  Response Time   : Within 30 days of receipt of a valid request

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article 9 (Amendments to Privacy Policy)

This Privacy Policy may be revised in accordance with changes in applicable law
or service. Revisions implemented without renewed consent will be announced at
least 7 days prior to the effective date via in-game notices or Steam
announcements.


=================
DOCUMENT CHANGE LOG
=================

v1.5 — Announced June 13, 2026 / Corresponds to server-based client Ver 1.00.12 and later
  Correction of v1.4 and move to consent-version-based application:
  - Reverted the amendment notice period from 3 days back to 7 days, and
    clarified that it applies only to amendments implemented without renewed
    consent and does not delay consent-based version changes (ToS Art. 3(2),
    3(5); Privacy Art. 9)
  - Switched the application basis from the client build to the version the
    User has consented to (recorded on the account); client version references
    are informational only. Users who have not consented to this version remain
    governed by the version to which they last consented; Users with no
    recorded consent must consent to the then-current version to use the Game
    (ToS Art. 3(4))
  - Added an explicit "Effect of declining consent" clause: declining limits
    use to offline / previous-version features, with server-stored items and
    online features unavailable; includes guidance on requesting a copy of
    personal data before declining (ToS Art. 3(6))
  - Corrected the Marketplace definition and transaction medium to Steam Wallet
    funds on the Steam Community Market (ToS Art. 2(6), 9(1), 10(4))
  - Added a joint controllers clause under GDPR Art. 26: Nugem Studio
    (distribution, operation, contact point) and Tesseract Studio
    (co-development), with roles set out (Privacy Art. 8)
  - Aligned SDK statements with actual practice: limited to "no advertising
    cookies or behavioral-analytics SDKs" and disclosed technical automatic
    collection necessary for operation and security (Privacy Art. 1; ToS
    Art. 15(3))
  - Defined a purpose-bounded "game progress data" category among game-data
    and cheat-detection collection items, with representative examples
    (character level, in-game currency balances, stage progress); new
    categories of a different nature will be announced via policy amendment
    (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Added consumer-rights preservation language to the item-value-change
    clauses (ToS Art. 6(2), 9(5))
  - Added governing-language clause (English prevails) and a link to archived
    previous versions (ToS Art. 15)

v1.4 — Announced June 11, 2026 / Effective June 15, 2026  (superseded by v1.5)
  Dedicated game server migration (Backnd) — data processing update:
  - Added processors: AFI Inc. (Backnd, Korea) and Google LLC (Apps Script, US)
    (Privacy Art. 3)
  - Disclosed data newly processed by the game server: Steam ID / App ID / auth
    ticket; IP & country, device & language, OS, nickname, access datetime;
    inventory & item-transaction data; client version (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Expanded cheat-detection data items to match actual logging (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Added retention & destruction rules incl. account withdrawal (immediate data
    deletion, personal info within 7 days), residual-record periodic deletion,
    and 30-day server request logs (Privacy Art. 4)
  - Clarified that the right to erasure (GDPR Art. 17) may be restricted for
    cheat-detection data for up to 1 year from detection, under Art. 17(3)(e) and
    legitimate interests, while access / restriction / objection rights remain
    available (Privacy Art. 4, Art. 6)
  - Added international transfer bases: Korea adequacy decision; EU-U.S. DPF / SCCs
    (Privacy Art. 5)
  - Updated ToS Art. 8 to reflect dedicated server processing of online features
  - Added mandatory-collection notice for online service login (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Shortened amendment notice period from 7 days to 3 days (reverted in v1.5)

v1.3 — Announced May 29, 2026 / Effective June 6, 2026
  Data collection disclosure corrected to reflect actual data minimization
  practice:
  - Rewrote "Information We Collect" to a two-tier model: only Steam ID is
    collected on first access; additional data is collected solely upon cheat
    detection (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Removed data categories that are not actually collected: marketplace
    transaction records, device/access information, bug reports & error logs
    (Privacy Art. 1, 3)
  - Narrowed processing purposes accordingly (Privacy Art. 2)
  - Updated retention periods to match (Privacy Art. 3)
  - Clarified that Steam Marketplace records are held by Valve, not the Company
    (Privacy Art. 4)

v1.2 — Announced May 28, 2026 / Effective June 5, 2026
  - Changed contact email to help@nugemstudio.com
  - Terms effective immediately upon first publication; subsequent amendments
    effective 7 days after announcement (ToS Art. 3; Privacy Art. 8)

v1.1 — May 28, 2026
  GDPR compliance update:
  - Added per-data-type legal bases (Privacy Art. 1)
  - Clarified consent mechanism as checkbox + confirm button (ToS Art. 3)
  - Revised data retention by data type (Privacy Art. 3)
  - Added data subject rights with GDPR article references (Privacy Art. 5)
  - Clarified Valve data-sharing scope and SCCs (Privacy Art. 4)
  - Added EU DPA complaint right (Privacy Art. 5-3)
  - Updated Minor definition for EU age thresholds (ToS Art. 2)
  - Added service termination data export notice (ToS Art. 12)
  - Added tracking/analytics SDK disclaimer (ToS Art. 15)

v1.0 — May 27, 2026
  Initial release.
